name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
۞ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُم مِّن ضَعْفٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ضَعْفٍ قُوَّةً ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ضَعْفًا وَشَيْبَةً ۚ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْقَدِيرُ (٥٤)
```

```english
It is Allah who created you from [a state of] weakness, then He gave you power after weakness. Then, after power, He ordained weakness and old age: He creates whatever He wishes, and He is the All-knowing, the All-powerful. (54)
```

```arabic
وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يُقْسِمُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ مَا لَبِثُوا۟ غَيْرَ سَاعَةٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كَانُوا۟ يُؤْفَكُونَ (٥٥)
```

```english
On the day when the Hour sets in, the guilty will swear that they had remained only for an hour. That is how they were used to lying [in the world]. (55)
```

```arabic
وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ وَٱلْإِيمَـٰنَ لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِى كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْبَعْثِ ۖ فَهَـٰذَا يَوْمُ ٱلْبَعْثِ وَلَـٰكِنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (٥٦)
```

```english
But those who were given knowledge and faith will say, ‘Certainly you remained in Allah’s Book until the Day of Resurrection. This is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know.’ (56)
```

```arabic
فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لَّا يَنفَعُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ (٥٧)
```

```english
On that day, the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them, nor will they be asked to propitiate [Allah]. (57)
```

```arabic
وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِى هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ ۚ وَلَئِن جِئْتَهُم بِـَٔايَةٍ لَّيَقُولَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا مُبْطِلُونَ (٥٨)
```

```english
Certainly we have drawn for mankind in this Quran every [kind of] parable. Indeed if you bring them a sign, the faithless will surely say, ‘You are nothing but fabricators!’ (58)
```

```arabic
كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٥٩)
```

```english
Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know. (59)
```

```arabic
فَٱصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ (٦٠)
```

```english
So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. And do not let yourself be upset by those who have no conviction. (60)
```

## (Commentary)

This is the fourth section of the verses, and like the one preceding it, it contains the conclusion of the Surah.

His saying, the Exalted: **اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ ضَعْفٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ضَعْفٍ قُوَّةً ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ضَعْفًا وَشَيْبَةً** “It is Allah who created you from [a state of] weakness, then He appointed strength after weakness, then after strength He appointed weakness and old age” (30:54), etc. Weakness (الضعف) and strength (القوة) are opposites. The preposition “from” (من) in His saying “from weakness” (من ضعف) denotes the starting point (الابتداء); that is, the beginning of your creation was from a state of weakness, meaning He began your existence as weak beings. Its referent, based on what the contrast suggests, is the beginning of infancy, although it could possibly apply to the sperm-drop (النطفة).

What is meant by “strength after weakness” is reaching full maturity (بلوغ الأشد), and by “weakness after strength” is senility (الشيخوخة); hence, “old age” (شيبة) is joined to it as an explanatory apposition (عطف تفسير). The use of the indefinite form (التنكير) for “weakness” (ضعف) and “strength” (قوة) is to indicate ambiguity (الإبهام) and the lack of a fixed measure, due to the variation among individuals in this regard.

His saying: **يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَاءُ** “He creates whatever He wishes” (30:54); that is, just as He willed weakness and created it, then strength after it and created it, then weakness after that and created it. In this, there is a most complete indication that the succession of these states is part of creation (الخلق). Since this transition from one state to another is, in its very essence, a creative governance (تدبير خلقا), it belongs solely to Allah, the Creator of all things. Thus, none of them can claim that this is part of the governance (التدبير) that traces back to the "god of man," for instance, as the idolaters (الوثنية) claim.

Then He concluded the speech by mentioning knowledge and power, saying: **وَهُوَ الْعَلِيمُ الْقَدِيرُ** “and He is the All-knowing, the All-powerful” (30:54).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يُقْسِمُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ مَا لَبِثُوا غَيْرَ سَاعَةٍ كَذَلِكَ كَانُوا يُؤْفَكُونَ** “And when the Hour sets in, the guilty will swear that they did not tarry but for an hour. Thus were they used to being deluded” (30:55). These verses are like an appendix to the preceding verses that enumerated the signs (الآيات) and proofs for His oneness, the Exalted, and the Resurrection (البعث). They also serve as a preparation and preamble (تمهيد وتوطئة) for the verse with which the Surah concludes. For when He had enumerated some of the signs and proofs, and indicated that they are not among those from whom faith is expected or whose belief is hoped for, He intended to clarify that they are in ignorance of the truth, perceiving truthful discourse as falsehood and clear signs as being detached from their significance. In this manner, they are deluded (يؤفكون), and they shall have no excuse to offer.

This delusion (الإفك) and turning away from truth to falsehood persists within them and accompanies them until the rising of the Hour. Thus, they will imagine that they did not tarry in their graves—between death and the Resurrection—except for an hour of a day. The matter of the Resurrection became confused for them just as every truth had been confused for them, such that they perceived it as falsehood.

Thus, His saying: **وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يُقْسِمُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ مَا لَبِثُوا غَيْرَ سَاعَةٍ** “And when the Hour sets in, the guilty will swear that they did not tarry but for an hour” (30:55) recounts the confusion they experience regarding the interval between the world and the Day of Resurrection, to the point that they imagine it to be one of the hours of the world.

His saying: **كَذَلِكَ كَانُوا يُؤْفَكُونَ** “Thus were they used to being deluded” (30:55); that is, they were turned away from the truth toward falsehood. They were called to the truth, and proofs and signs were established for it, yet they imagined it to be false speech and superstitious opinion.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ وَالْإِيمَانَ لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْبَعْثِ فَهَذَا يَوْمُ الْبَعْثِ** “But those who were given knowledge and faith will say, ‘Certainly you have tarried in Allah’s Book until the Day of Resurrection. So this is the Day of Resurrection...’” (30:56), etc. This is a rebuttal from them to the claim of the guilty: “they did not tarry but for an hour.” For the guilty, due to their attachment to the earth and their immersion in the life of this world, view the Day of Resurrection and the interval between it and the world as being governed by the system of the world. Thus, they estimated the interval as an hour—a small amount of time—as if they thought they were still in the world, for that is the extent of their knowledge.

The people of knowledge and faith refuted them, stating that the tarrying is measured by the interval between the world and the Day of Resurrection. This is the interval referred to by His saying: **وَمِنْ وَرَائِهِمْ بَرْزَخٌ إِلَى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ** “and ahead of them is a barrier until the day they will be resurrected” (23:100).

From this, they concluded that this day is the Day of Resurrection. However, since the guilty were in doubt regarding the Resurrection and had no certainty (يقين) in anything other than the world, they imagined that only an hour of the world's hours had passed over them. This is the meaning of their saying: **لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْبَعْثِ فَهَذَا يَوْمُ الْبَعْثِ وَلَكِنَّكُمْ كُنْتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ** “Certainly you have tarried in Allah’s Book until the Day of Resurrection. So this is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know” (30:56); that is, you were ignorant and doubting, having no certainty in this day, and for that reason, the matter of the tarrying became confused for you.

From here, it becomes clear that what is meant by “those who were given knowledge and faith” (أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ وَالْإِيمَانَ) is certainty (اليقين) and commitment to its requirements. Knowledge (العلم) here means certainty in Allah and His signs, and faith (الإيمان) means the commitment to the requirements of that certainty, both being divine gifts. It also becomes clear that “Allah’s Book” (كتاب الله) refers to the heavenly scriptures or specifically the Quran, and nothing else. The view of some who say there is a transposition (تقديم وتأخير) in the verse—and that the meaning is: “And those who were given knowledge and faith said regarding Allah’s Book: You have tarried until the Day of Resurrection”—is of no account.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لَا يَنْفَعُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ** “On that day the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them, nor will they be asked to offer any apology” (30:57). **Seeking favor** (الاستعتاب) is the request for goodwill (العتبى), and **goodwill** (العتبى) is the removal of blame. That is, their excuse for their wrongdoing will not benefit them, nor will they be asked to remove the blame from themselves.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِي هَذَا الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ** “Certainly We have drawn for mankind in this Quran every [kind of] parable...” (30:58), etc. This is an allusion to their being deluded and turned away from the truth, such that no parable that brings the truth close to their hearts benefits them, because their hearts have been sealed. For this reason, He followed it with His saying: **وَلَئِنْ جِئْتَهُمْ بِآيَةٍ لَيَقُولَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا مُبْطِلُونَ** “...and if you bring them a sign, the faithless will surely say, ‘You are nothing but fabricators’” (30:58); that is, you have brought falsehood. This statement comes from them because they are turned away from the truth, seeing every truth as falsehood. The use of the relative pronoun and its clause (الموصول والصلة) in place of a simple pronoun is to indicate the reason for their statement.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **كَذَلِكَ يَطْبَعُ اللَّهُ عَلَى قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ** “Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know” (30:59); that is, those who are ignorant of Allah and His signs, including the Resurrection, and who persist in their ignorance and doubt.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **فَاصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَلَا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ** “So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. And do not let yourself be upset by those who have no conviction” (30:60); that is, be patient with what they confront you with of their saying: “You are nothing but fabricators,” and their other mockeries. Indeed, Allah’s promise that He will grant you victory is true, as He alluded to in His saying: **وَكَانَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا نَصْرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ** “and it was a must for Us to help the believers” (30:47). And do not let those who have no conviction (لا يوقنون) in the promise of Allah, the Glorified, unsettle you.

The view of some who say the meaning is “they have no conviction in the clear signs you recite to them, by their denial of them and their harming you with their falsehoods,” is of no consequence. The Surah began with a promise and concluded with a promise, and both promises are for victory.
